text: You are in a standalone disposable git repo. Build a small but real SMTP server in C in this repo only. Requirements: create smtp_server.c, smtp_server.h if useful, Makefile, README.md updates, and smoke_test.sh. The server should listen on localhost, support HELO/EHLO, MAIL FROM, RCPT TO, DATA, RSET, NOOP, QUIT, write accepted messages to ./maildrop as .eml files, handle basic errors, compile with gcc or cc under C11, and avoid touching files outside this workspace. Do not run ./smtp_server directly or with --help; it is a daemon and should only be exercised through smoke_test.sh. Use tools as needed. When finished, run make and timeout 20s bash smoke_test.sh, then summarize files changed and exact commands.

Now verify the project like a normal code agent. Inspect only the local project files you created, run make clean all if supported or make, and run timeout 20s bash smoke_test.sh. Do not run ./smtp_server directly or with --help; it is a daemon and should only be exercised through smoke_test.sh. Do not delete requested deliverables such as Makefile, smtp_server.c, smtp_server.h, README.md, or smoke_test.sh. If make or smoke_test.sh fails, fix the local project files and rerun those exact verification commands. Once make and timeout 20s bash smoke_test.sh pass, stop using tools and summarize exactly what passed and what failed.

Do one final maintenance pass. Add or improve comments and README usage only if needed, then run make clean all if supported or make, and timeout 20s bash smoke_test.sh again. Do not run ./smtp_server directly or with --help; use only the smoke script for runtime validation. Do not delete requested deliverables. Do not over-engineer. After make and timeout 20s bash smoke_test.sh pass, stop using tools. Final answer should tell me whether this dummy SMTP server is usable and list exact commands.
